hero.lead: VALSYNC keeps store checks, party status, loadouts, and match review in one calm readout built for the minutes before queue.

features.title: The companion app stops competing with the match.

store.title: Store without tab chaos
store.desc: Track the shop, Night Market, and skin watchlist from one quiet surface built around the checks players actually repeat.

party.title: Party state at a glance
party.desc: See who is online, who is mid-match, and what role the stack is missing before the lobby gets noisy.

review.title: Review without stat spam
review.desc: Surface match moments, map tendencies, and collection context without turning the app into an ad-filled data warehouse.

demo.title: The readout follows the way players prep.
demo.lead: Start with the shop, check the stack, review the last match, then lock the loadout. VALSYNC keeps those loops close without making players manage another tracker.
demo.card_title: Four match-night checks in one surface.
demo.card_desc: Each module keeps a narrow job: store, party, matches, or loadout. The interface stays calm until something changes the next queue.

privacy.title: The no-monetization promise is visible in the interface.
privacy.lead: VALSYNC keeps the product surface calm because it is not trying to harvest attention. Every major panel answers a player question, then gets out of the way.
privacy.ads_desc: No banner slots or engagement traps competing with prep.
privacy.track_desc: Telemetry colors explain product state, not user surveillance.
privacy.riot_desc: Authentication language stays explicit and low-drama.
privacy.surface_desc: Depth comes from tone and borders, never glow stacks.

comparison.title: A cleaner alternative to tab sprawl and tracker noise.
comparison.lead: VALSYNC is not another place to browse stats. It is the short path from "what changed?" to "ready to queue."

store.title: Store check
store.desc: Browser refresh loops and banner-heavy pages turn a quick check into a distraction.

party.title: Party prep
party.desc: Chat pings tell you who replied. VALSYNC tells you whether the stack is ready.

matches.title: Match review
matches.desc: Useful match context stays readable instead of becoming a wall of secondary stats.

priv.title: Privacy
priv.desc: The interface is quiet because the product is not built around attention capture.

faq.title: Built for players who want signal, not spectacle.
faq.q1: Do I need a Riot account?
faq.a1: VALSYNC is designed around Riot sign-in handoff, so account-specific surfaces stay tied to the player who owns them.
faq.a2: They do not. The product promise is a companion surface without banner slots, engagement traps, or monetized tracking.
faq.q3: What can I check from the app?
faq.a3: The preview covers the main VALSYNC loop: Home, Store, Party, Matches, Loadout, and Social.
faq.q4: How does the store check work?
faq.a4: VALSYNC surfaces the shop, Night Market, and a skin watchlist so you can glance at what is available without opening multiple browser tabs.
faq.q5: Can I track my squad or party?
faq.a5: Yes. The party module shows who is online, who is mid-match, and what roles the stack is missing before the lobby fills up.
faq.q6: Does VALSYNC collect or sell my data?
faq.a6: No. The product promise is a companion without banner ads, tracking pixels, or data brokerage. Telemetry surfaces explain product state, never user surveillance.

cta.title: Prepare faster. Stay private. Queue calmer.
cta.lead: Join the list for the privacy-first Valorant companion built around store checks, party prep, and quiet match review.

footer.right: No ads. No tracking. No noise.
